events.name: Into the Hollow — north mouth
events.description: The rune-stones flare; the gate is a doorway.

kind.flavor: You step through the North Gate into a cavern of pale rock.

events.name: Into the Hollow — east mouth
events.description: The rune-stones hum as you cross the threshold.

kind.flavor: You step through the East Gate. The Hollow opens before you.

events.name: Into the Hollow — south mouth
events.description: The arch shudders, then stills.

kind.flavor: You step through the South Gate. The air thickens, dust and old breath.

events.name: Into the Hollow — west mouth
events.description: The weathered arch parts at your touch.

kind.flavor: You step through the West Gate. The Hollow stretches east and south.

events.name: Survivors' Camp
events.description: A handful of survivors huddle around a fire, clutching torn maps.

kind.speaker: Elder Marwen
lines: You walk these roads now? Brave. Or fool. The same, these days.
The Frost Lord was the lid on a kettle. You smashed it, and now the kettle boils.
Four castles. Four lords. Lords that should still be sleeping.
If you mean to set this right — and the only ones who can still walk are the ones who must — start with the cold one.
Castle of Frost, north along the road. But its gates are sealed.
Find Hael at the old spire, west of here. He owes me a favour and he likes a long walk.

outcomes.text: Find Hael at the Spire of Hael, west of here.

events.name: Spire of Hael
events.description: Smoke curls from the cracked window. A wiry old man peers down.

kind.speaker: Hael the Hedge-Wizard
lines: Marwen sent you. Of course she did. She's been sending everyone since the sky changed.
Listen — the Frost Castle's seal is keyed to Starstone fragments. I have one. It's yours.
But fashioning a key takes effort, and effort takes pacing. Walk the long road and come back. The further you wander, the truer the key cuts.
Bring me distance and I'll bring you a way in.
Walk two kilometers along the chaos roads. The corrupted will test you. Don't die.
Come back to me when you've earned the steps.

outcomes.text: Walk 2 km in the chaos lands, then return to Hael.

events.name: Hael's Promise Kept
events.description: Hael holds out a pale-blue key, breath misting in the cold spire air.

kind.speaker: Hael the Hedge-Wizard
lines: You walked. You bled, by the look of those boots. Good.
Here. The Frostbound Key. It will open the outer gate of the cold one's castle.
Inside you'll find the Queen of Frost. She was the first of the four to wake.
She remembers her old name and resents the new world. Go gently if you can. Strike if you cannot.
Either way — walk back here when she's done. There are three more castles after this one.

outcomes.text: Received: Frostbound Key. The Castle of Frost is to the north.

events.name: Castle of Frost — Sealed
events.description: The gate hums with old magic. It won't open without a key.

kind.speaker: Voice from the Gate
lines: Cold seeps from every joint of the stone.
The gate is sealed. A key shaped like a splinter of starlight would fit here.
You should find Hael at the old spire. He'll know what to do.

outcomes.text: The gate is sealed. Seek Hael at the Spire of Hael.

events.name: East Gate Scout
events.description: A weathered scout warming her hands at the rune-stones.

kind.speaker: Scout Lirien
lines: You walked all the way out here? Then you've seen what's wrong with the world.
I've been watching the Castle of Flame from this gate. The forge inside is melted shut — fused itself like a wound trying to scar.
Found this in the ash-line near the keep. Copper, but it stays warm. The Lord of Flame fears it, I think.
Take it. Crack that door. Bring me back the Lord's quiet, if you can.

outcomes.text: Received: Ember Talisman. The Castle of Flame is to the north.

events.name: Castle of Flame — Sealed
events.description: The basalt gate has welded itself shut. Heat shimmers off the stone.

kind.speaker: Voice from the Forge
lines: The door is one continuous molten weld. No pry-bar will open it.
Scouts at the East Gate have been watching this place. One of them might have something that could crack it.

outcomes.text: The gate is forge-sealed. Visit the East Gate scout.

events.name: Lord of Flame
events.description: The talisman flares against the door — it splits with a crack like a struck anvil. Heat washes out.

dialogue_intro: Mortal. You felt the rim of the talisman and thought yourself ready.
I have been Lord of every forge that ever cooled. Each pause between hammer-strokes — that was me, gathering.
And now you give me an excuse to wake the iron in your blood.
dialogue_defeat: The forge banks itself. The iron cools.
Walk well, mortal. Two remain — and one of them does not burn.

outcomes.text: Lord of Flame defeated! Dropped: Fire Blade. Boon: Forge-Tempered. Two castles quiet. Two remain.

events.name: South Gate Hermit
events.description: A wiry figure sits cross-legged by the rune-stones, a black-glass lantern unlit at her side.

kind.speaker: Hermit Sora
lines: I came south to be alone with the quiet. Now the quiet has teeth.
Beyond this gate, the Castle of Shadow draws a shroud across itself. You will walk up to it and find you are walking away.
Take my lantern. It burns inward — the void-light. Carry it and the shroud will lift.
Bring back the silence of that place. Whatever silence the Hierophant has left.

outcomes.text: Received: Voidlight Lantern. The Castle of Shadow is to the south-west.

events.name: Castle of Shadow — Veiled
events.description: Each step you take toward the castle, the path bends you away from it. The shroud is hungry.

kind.speaker: Voice from the Shroud
lines: The way is veiled. You cannot find the door from outside the door.
The South Gate hermit speaks of an anti-light. Without it the shroud will turn you home, again and again.

outcomes.text: The shroud turns you away. Visit the South Gate hermit.

events.name: Hierophant of Shadow
events.description: The lantern flares — the shroud parts. A hooded figure rises from a half-collapsed throne of obsidian.

dialogue_intro: You carry a candle into a cathedral and call it bravery.
I am Hierophant of every word never said aloud. I am the quiet you fled to make this noise.
Step forward. I will teach you the silence you were too afraid of.
dialogue_defeat: Curious. The candle was bigger than the cathedral.
Walk on, mortal. The storm-bound is the last.

outcomes.text: Hierophant of Shadow defeated! Dropped: Dragonslayer. Boon: Voidsight. Three castles quiet. One remains.

events.name: West Gate Wanderer
events.description: A traveler with charred boots and a copper-braided wrist-cord squints up at you.

kind.speaker: Wanderer Cael
lines: I was a fisherman. Then the cliffs east of here started ringing with thunder, and a castle stood where no castle stood.
Storm Castle. Lightning falls on anyone who climbs the cliff path.
I made this — copper and yew, the old way. Wear it and the bolts will pass around you. They won't strike a thing that won't conduct.
If you make it inside, end the Stormbinder for me. He was the storm I never saw coming.

outcomes.text: Received: Grounding Charm. The Castle of Storm is to the east.

events.name: Castle of Storm — Lightning-Ringed
events.description: The cliff path is hammered with bolts. You won't take three steps before one finds you.

kind.speaker: Voice from the Cliffs
lines: Lightning crowns this place. Without a way to ground the strikes, the climb will kill you.
The West Gate wanderer makes charms that let the storm pass around you. Find him first.

outcomes.text: Lightning forbids the path. Visit the West Gate wanderer.

events.name: Stormbinder
events.description: The charm hums against your wrist as you climb. At the keep's heart, a figure turns from the open sky, lightning crowning his head.

dialogue_intro: You came up the cliff with copper at your wrist. Clever, mortal.
I bound the storm to me when the boundary broke. The bolts go where I tell them, except — apparently — where you stand.
Then we will see what falls when the storm cannot reach you.
dialogue_defeat: Four castles quiet. Walk to the centre. The Starstone is waiting for the second cut.

outcomes.text: Stormbinder defeated! Dropped: Stormbringer. Boon: Lightning-Footed. Four castles quiet. The Starstone awaits.

events.name: Queen of Frost
events.description: She turns from the throne, crown of icicles gleaming. The temperature plummets.

dialogue_intro: I was made to rule the cold places — and you woke me.
Tell me, walker — who sent you? Marwen? Hael? The Starstone itself?
It does not matter. The boundary is broken. I will be the cold that closes it.
dialogue_defeat: Curious. You walked all the way here just to end me again.

outcomes.text: Queen of Frost defeated! Dropped: Frost Axe. Boon: Frostproof. One castle quiet. Three remain.

events.name: Camp Quartermaster
events.description: A stout woman behind a plank-and-barrel counter. Lanterns, blankets, scuffed weapons, the smell of dried meat.

events.name: Forgemaster Vorn
events.description: A bearded smith bent over a battered anvil. He looks up, hammer mid-swing, and grunts a greeting.

events.name: Wandering Apothecary
events.description: A weathered woman with herb-stained fingers sets out vials on a folded cloak.

events.name: Black Market
events.description: Half-collapsed ruins, a hooded figure under the broken arch. He doesn't lift his head. "Browse. Don't ask names."

events.name: Town Watch
events.description: A watchman in dented chainmail glances up from sharpening a notched sword.

kind.speaker: Watchman Edgar
lines: You've come a long way for a stranger. Marwen sends them through here sometimes.
The roads aren't safe. Bandits on the easier paths, knights on the harder ones, things in the woods I can't name.
Drink from the well if you stop. Don't sleep outside the palisade. And don't trust the silence near the castles.

outcomes.text: Watchman Edgar shares a meal and points you down the road.

events.name: Mountain Village Elder
events.description: An old woman with a fur-trimmed shawl sits on a porch overlooking the cliffs.

kind.speaker: Elder Yska
lines: We saw the Castle of Storm rise. It came out of the cliffs like a tooth pushing through gum.
The lightning crowns it every midnight. We've lost two goats and a goatherd to it.
If you mean to climb up there — wait for a still hour. And cover your wrists with copper.

outcomes.text: Elder Yska presses a torch into your hand. "For the dark places."

events.name: Refugee Carver
events.description: A wood-carver among a knot of refugees, hands working a chip of pine into a tiny figure.

kind.speaker: Carver Liso
lines: We came from the south. The Castle of Shadow took our village in a single night — woke up to mist where the orchard had been.
I carve these as charms now. Take one. It won't ward off the dark, but it's company.
If you find the Hierophant — make him answer for what was done.

outcomes.text: A wooden charm from Carver Liso. He offers a potion too.

events.name: Forest Hunter
events.description: A green-clad hunter cleaning a longbow at the village edge.

kind.speaker: Hunter Vex
lines: Tracks I don't know. Hooves the wrong size, no smell I can place. Beasts the woods didn't have last spring.
I trap what I can; the rest I let pass. If you walk east toward the Flame Castle, the wolves are worse — corrupted, all of them.
Take a draught. The world's bigger than your legs make it.

outcomes.text: Hunter Vex shares a haste draught and points east.

events.name: The Archaeologist
events.description: A young scholar kneels in the rubble, brushing soil from a fragment of dark stone.

kind.speaker: Scholar Tessa
lines: These ruins are older than the chaos. Someone built here before the Starstone fell — and someone tore it down.
The script is broken, but I can read one word over and over: 'bound.' The Starstone wasn't always loose, walker.
Here, take this. I copied the safe parts of the inscription. It's a map — sort of.

outcomes.text: Scholar Tessa hands you a copied fragment — an Explorer's Map.

events.name: The Mad Prospector
events.description: An old man crouches at the cave mouth, sorting pebbles by some private logic.

kind.speaker: Prospector Halby
lines: Hssh — quiet. They're sleeping. The veins, I mean. The cave veins. Pulse if you touch the wrong wall.
Found a purse though! Some traveler left it. Or didn't leave it. Hard to say, with the dark.
Here, take half. I don't spend coin anymore. Coin makes the veins itch.

outcomes.text: Prospector Halby splits a found purse with you. +150 gold.

events.name: The Edge-of-the-Map Hermit
events.description: A weather-stained cabin at the southern frontier. The hermit waves you in like he's been expecting you.

kind.speaker: Hermit Olund
lines: Heard the wind change last week. Means another castle stirring — they don't all rise at once, you know.
Sit by the fire a moment. I've walked the south paths since before the chaos. Let me give you the lay.
Reveal: south coast, west bog, east road. There. Now the map in your head matches the world.

outcomes.text: Hermit Olund traces the south paths on your map — fog clears in 3 patches.

events.name: Pilgrim at the Shrine
events.description: A robed figure prays at the moss-covered shrine, then turns slowly as you approach.

kind.speaker: Pilgrim Aren
lines: Stranger. The shrine's old god is gone — the Starstone took the prayers when it cracked.
But the shrine still listens. Leave your name in it. Or your fear. The stone keeps the secret.
Take this with you. Pilgrims carry it for the long walk. May your road be soft.

outcomes.text: Pilgrim Aren blesses your road. A speed potion changes hands.

events.name: Scholar in the Tower
events.description: A young scholar climbs out of a trapdoor with a stack of soot-blackened books.

kind.speaker: Scholar Maren
lines: Walker! Good — I've been catalogueing chaos-touched relics. You walking ones see them first.
If you find anything unusual in the castles, bring it to me later. For now — this is for the journey.
It's not magic. It's just a very good rope. But you'll be surprised how often that's the better answer.

outcomes.text: Scholar Maren gifts you rope and a compass for the long walks.

events.name: Refugee Camp
events.description: A handful of tents around a smoking pit. Children chase each other; a woman is mending boots.

kind.speaker: Refugee Mira
lines: There's nothing to spare, but you've a kind face. Sit with us a moment.
We came from the village by the lake — the lake that wasn't there before. We don't know where we're going.
If you find anyone who knows how to send the lake back, tell them we're here.

outcomes.text: Refugee Mira hands you a warm cloak. "You'll need it more than us."

events.name: Void Imps in the Trees
events.description: Twigs snap. Half a dozen pinprick eyes blink from the canopy.

kind.description: A handful of imps drop from the branches!

outcomes.text: Scattered the imps! +60 gold, +1 Health Potion.

events.name: Corrupted Wolf Pack
events.description: The undergrowth shifts. Three shapes with eyes the wrong color slip into the clearing.

outcomes.text: Drove off the wolves! +80 gold, +1 Haste Draught.

events.name: Stone-Bound Raider
events.description: A figure rises from behind a boulder — silent, plate-armored, eyes like cold lamps.

kind.description: The raider charges, axe in both hands!

outcomes.text: Felled the raider! +120 gold. Dropped: Iron Sword.

events.name: Bog Wraith
events.description: The mist gathers waist-high, then thickens into a shape with arms.

kind.description: The wraith lunges, mist clinging like cobweb!

outcomes.text: Dispersed the wraith! +100 gold. Dropped: Bog Charm.

events.name: Road Bandit Ambush
events.description: Three figures step out from the long grass, blades drawn. "Toll. Make it generous."

kind.description: Three bandits surround you on the road!

outcomes.text: Routed the bandits! +70 gold. Dropped: Leather Vest.

events.name: Hidden Coin Pouch
events.description: A leather pouch half-buried at the base of a split tree.

outcomes.text: Found a hidden pouch — +90 gold.

events.name: Refugee Cache
events.description: A small chest hidden under brush, abandoned by someone in a hurry.

kind.description: Survival kit a refugee buried before fleeing.

outcomes.text: Refugee cache: +60 gold, +1 Health Potion, +1 Torch.

events.name: Bandit Stash
events.description: Shovel-marks in the dirt. Whoever buried this didn't have time to come back.

kind.description: Bandits buried this hoard before something found them.

outcomes.text: Bandit stash: +150 gold, +Leather Boots.

events.name: Tarnished Reliquary
events.description: A small reliquary on a moss-grown plinth. The inside is dry, untouched by rain.

kind.description: A pilgrim's leftover stash, intact for some reason no one wants to ask about.

outcomes.text: Reliquary opened — +120 gold, +Ring of Vigor.

events.name: Sunken Saddle-bag
events.description: A saddle-bag in the grass — its horse long gone, contents miraculously dry.

kind.description: Whoever rode through here was carrying a fortune.

outcomes.text: Saddle-bag: +200 gold, +Speed Potion.

events.name: The Vista
events.description: The trees break. You can see for kilometers — the whole north of the chaos lands laid out.

lines: From this rise the world stretches out: forest to mountain to the curve of distant cliffs.
You can see the Castle of Frost in the north, a glint of ice. The smoke from the Castle of Flame far east.
The wound in the world has shape from up here. Almost a map.

outcomes.text: Vista — north and east revealed on your map.

events.name: Old Battle Site
events.description: Bone-bleached weapons, a few shields warped by fire. No bodies — they were carried away or worse.

lines: Someone made a stand here. Recently. The grass is just starting to come back.
You pick up a shield half-melted at the rim. The arms etched on it aren't any kingdom you know.
It was a refugee militia, you think. They didn't make it to the camp.

outcomes.text: Battle site — +40 gold, salvaged Leather Vest.

events.name: The Standing Stone
events.description: A standing stone, taller than you. Runes spiral up its face — the same word, over and over.

lines: BOUND. BOUND. BOUND. All the way up the stone.
Someone meant to remind the world of something. Or someone.
The stone is warm under your palm — like it's still working.

outcomes.text: The standing stone hums faintly under your palm. (+30 gold for the rubbings you take.)

events.name: The Starstone Reveals Itself
events.description: The campfire stills. Above it, between the tents, a wound in the air opens — and through it, light.

lines: Four castles quiet. Four lords ended.
The wound is yours to close, walker. The Starstone is here — it has always been here — but it would not show its face until the lords were silent.
Bring the four blades to this fire. The second cut waits for you.

outcomes.text: Bring all four boss weapons to the Survivors' Camp.

events.name: Echo of the First Cut
events.description: You lay the four blades at the fire's edge. They shake. The light above the camp coalesces into a figure — your own shape, but older, wearier, holding the same four weapons.

dialogue_intro: Walker. You think the second cut is a kindness?
I made the first one. I sealed the boundary. I have been the wound ever since.
Strike me and I am undone. The wound closes. The world quiets.
Or do not — and walk away, and let the next four lords rise.
dialogue_defeat: The wound is sewn. Walk back into the loud world, traveler. You earned the noise.

outcomes.text: Echo of the First Cut severed. The chaos lands quiet. You receive the Starstone Shard and the Starstone Awakened boon.
